seoTitle: Speed Up or Slow Down Video in Canva (Desktop/Mobile)
metaDescription: Learn how to speed up or slow down video clips in Canva on both desktop and mobile, so you can highlight key moments in social and marketing content.
intro: Adjusting how fast a clip plays is a simple way to add emphasis to a video, whether you're speeding through a transition or slowing down a key moment. This guide walks through changing video speed in Canva on both desktop and mobile, so the same technique works no matter which device you're editing on. It's aimed at anyone creating social content, promotional videos, or presentations that need better pacing.

steps.heading: Open the video speed controls
steps.body: Select your video clip in Canva and access the speed adjustment feature, available on both desktop and mobile.

steps.heading: Speed up your video clip
steps.body: Increase the playback speed on a clip to condense footage or create a faster-paced moment.

steps.heading: Slow down your video clip
steps.body: Reduce the playback speed to emphasize a specific moment or improve clarity for the viewer.

steps.heading: Apply the adjustment to your storytelling
steps.body: Use the speed changes intentionally across your project so the pacing supports the story you're telling.

faq.question: Does Canva's video speed feature work the same on mobile and desktop?
faq.answer: Yes, this tutorial shows the process on both desktop and mobile so you can adjust speed regardless of which device you use.

faq.question: Can I speed up only part of a video clip?
faq.answer: The speed adjustment applies to the selected clip, so you can apply different speeds to different clips within your project.

faq.question: Why would I slow down a video in Canva?
faq.answer: Slowing down footage can highlight a critical moment or create a more dynamic storytelling effect in presentations and social content.

faq.question: Is this feature useful for social media content specifically?
faq.answer: Yes, adjusting video speed is commonly used for engaging social media content and promotional videos.
